neighborhoods.name: Downtown Asheville
neighborhoods.description: Vibrant Art Deco downtown with craft breweries, farm-to-table restaurants, and street performers
neighborhoods.character: Art Deco, craft beer capital, farm-to-table

housing.predominantType: Loft apartments, condos, and renovated historic buildings

neighborhoods.character_notes: The historic and commercial heart of Asheville. Most walkable area with best access to cultural attractions, restaurants, and public services.

neighborhoods.name: West Asheville
neighborhoods.description: Rapidly gentrifying neighborhood across the river with Haywood Road's local shops and restaurants
neighborhoods.character_notes: Established residential area offering a quieter lifestyle while maintaining reasonable access to Asheville's center and services.

neighborhoods.name: Weaverville / Swannanoa
neighborhoods.description: Small towns north and east of Asheville with mountain character and lower housing costs
neighborhoods.character_notes: More affordable option for budget-conscious retirees. May require a car or longer transit times to reach central amenities.

neighborhoods.name: South Asheville / Biltmore Area
neighborhoods.description: Area near the Biltmore Estate with family-friendly neighborhoods, parks, and mountain views

housing.predominantType: Single-family homes, newer developments, and townhomes

neighborhoods.character_notes: Family-friendly area with good schools, parks, and community amenities. Popular with active retirees and families.
